items.name: Burrata Artigianale
items.description: Hand-crafted burrata from local farms, heirloom tomatoes, basil oil.
items.story: Our burrata is made each morning by Maria's cousin in Andria — the birthplace of burrata. The milk comes from bufala raised on the Murge plateau. It is pulled by hand, shaped into its signature form, and delivered before noon. We serve it with tomatoes from our garden and oil pressed last October.

items.name: Polpo Arrostito
items.description: Charred octopus, smoked potato puree, Taggiasca olives, caper powder.
items.story: Each morning, Silve visits the Fasano fish market. The octopus is first tenderized against dock rocks — an ancient technique passed down through generations — then slow-cooked for four hours before being seared over open fire. The smoke is part of the dish's soul.

items.name: Orecchiette al Pomodoro
items.description: Handmade orecchiette, roasted cherry tomatoes, cacioricotta, fresh basil.
items.story: Orecchiette — 'little ears' — are Puglia's soul. Our nonna Rosa makes them by hand each afternoon, pressing each one with her thumb in a gesture she has repeated for sixty years. The tomatoes are from our garden, slow-roasted until their sweetness concentrates into something profound.

items.name: Risotto ai Frutti di Mare
items.description: Acquerello rice, fresh Adriatic seafood, lemon zest, saffron.
items.story: This risotto is made only when the sea is right. We use Acquerello rice aged for one year, stirred for eighteen minutes without pause. Saffron from Abruzzo blooms in warm broth. Clams and scampi arrive still breathing from the morning catch. We finish it with lemon from our terrace tree.

items.name: Pescato del Giorno
items.description: Catch of the day, baked in salt crust or grilled with seasonal vegetables.
items.story: We do not know what we will serve until Silve returns from the market. Some mornings it is orata, others branzino or spigola. The salt crust method, taught to Silve by his grandfather, seals every molecule of moisture inside the fish. It steams in its own essence for thirty minutes.

items.name: Filetto di Manzo
items.description: Beef tenderloin, Primitivo reduction, roasted wild mushrooms.
items.story: We source our beef from a small farm near Alberobello, where cattle graze on Valle d'Itria pastures. The Primitivo wine reduction takes three hours and collapses into a dark velvet. The mushrooms are foraged from the Murge hills the morning of service. Nothing here is incidental.
